case_results.description: Create a new account with zero initial balance.
Rename an account and keep account information available under the new name.
Add an expense transaction and update the account balance.
Add an income transaction and update the account balance.
Edit a transaction amount and refresh account balance.
Delete a transaction and remove its effect from account balance.
Create a category group and a category under the group.
Assign a transaction to a category.
Set a monthly budget for a category.
Calculate category spent amount and budget balance.
Generate a simple monthly report with income and expense totals.
Calculate net worth from account balances.
Create a scheduled transaction definition.
Create a rule that sets category based on payee.
Reuse a payee and assign a tag to a transaction.
Reject a transaction without an account and keep state unchanged.
Account balance updates after multiple linked transactions.
A categorized transaction updates category spending and budget balance.
A transaction updates monthly report totals and net worth.
Multiple expenses accumulate correctly in account, budget, and report.
Monthly budget and report only count transactions from the selected month.
Account balance, category spending, budget balance, and report totals agree for one expense.
Editing a transaction refreshes account balance, budget values, and report values.
A failed transaction without account does not change existing state.
A transaction with both payment and deposit greater than zero fails without changing state.

command_results.stderr: error: transaction cannot have both payment and deposit

check_results.actual: error: transaction cannot have both payment and deposit

case_results.description: A rule created before a matching transaction automatically categorizes it when rules are applied.
A rule created after an existing transaction can update that transaction when rules are applied.
A schedule generates a real transaction that updates account balance and report values.
A schedule-generated transaction can be categorized by a rule and then update budget and report values.
Renaming a category preserves related transactions, budgets, and report consistency.
